summary: Large decompilation pass for Fox/Falco/Kirby blaster item logic, replacing an effectively empty src/melee/it/items/itfoxblaster.c with about 980 lines of C plus the headers needed to type item vars and fighter/Kirby callbacks. The PR body explicitly said "Not 100% diff yet", and there were no review comments, so this record should be treated as evidence of an intermediate-but-merged matching attempt rather than a fully polished decomp.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; added item state table, constants, spawn/clear/update/accessory/collision/destruction-style functions for Fox/Falco/Kirby blaster items.
Added FoxBlasterVars with offset-commented fields from xDD4 through xE7C, including Vec3 trails/positions and owner HSD_GObj pointer.
Inserted FoxBlasterVars foxblaster into Item.xDD4_itemVar union before existing FoxLaserVars.
Added ftFx_SpecialNIndex enum mapping special-N action subindices, with comments tying values to currASID offsets.
Corrected Kirby special-N/Fox-copy helper prototypes to include Vec3 out parameters, bool returns, and ftFx_SpecialNIndex return type.
Added clang-format pre-commit hook using mirrors-clang-format v18.1.8.

what_changed: Added a full C implementation skeleton/pass for itfoxblaster.c, including includes for fighter, item, effect, JObj, and refract dependencies.

Declared and defined item state table it_803F6CA8 with 11 entries wiring callbacks such as it_802AEBCC, it_802AEED4, it_802AEF08, it_802AEF10, it_802AEFB8, it_802AEFBC, it_802AF064, it_802AF178, and it_802AF17C.
Added data tables used for JObj transforms and state mapping, including it_803F6D58, it_803F6D90, it_803F6DC8, it_803F6E28, it_803F6E3C, and it_803F6E68.

Implemented blaster item behavior around visibility/flags, accessory updates, muzzle/effect position capture, recoil/visual animation stepping, spawn initialization, owner tracking, action-state synchronization, cleanup, and scale update logic.
Handled both normal Fox/Falco blasters and Kirby copy variants by switching on item kinds It_Kind_Fox_Blaster, It_Kind_Falco_Blaster, It_Kind_Kirby_FoxBlaster, and It_Kind_Kirby_FalcoBlaster.
Added FoxBlasterVars to itCharItems.h and linked it into the Item union in it/types.h so the decompiled code could access item->xDD4_itemVar.foxblaster fields.
Added ftFx_SpecialNIndex enum values for Start, Loop, End, AirStart, AirLoop, AirEnd, ThrowB, ThrowHi, and ThrowLw.
Updated Kirby special-N function declarations to reflect actual signatures used by the blaster item code, including Vec3* output arguments and bool return values.
Added a repository-level pre-commit clang-format configuration.
smart_moves: Introduced a dedicated FoxBlasterVars struct instead of leaving all xDD4_itemVar accesses untyped, making the large item implementation searchable and easier to refine later.
Used ftFx_SpecialNIndex as a shared semantic type for Fox/Kirby special-N state mapping, reducing magic-number-only action handling.
Kept offset comments on the new FoxBlasterVars fields, which is important for item union layout verification and future field naming.
Separated normal fighter and Kirby-copy helper calls while preserving shared item behavior, which exposes the parallel structure between ftFx_SpecialN and ftKb_SpecialNFx routines.
Added small helper logic such as jobj_child and clear_blaster to reduce repeated null-sensitive JObj/cleanup patterns, even though some surrounding code remained rough.
Preserved decomp/matching clues in comments, including M2C uncertainty, alternative attempted code shapes, and notes about JObj transform tables.
decomp_lessons: For character-owned items, decomp progress often requires simultaneous header work in fighter code and item var typing; itfoxblaster.c needed ftFox forward declarations, Kirby prototype fixes, and Item union updates.
Action-state enum extraction can clarify item animation tables: ftFx_SpecialNIndex values were documented as offsets from currASID relative to ftFx_MS_SpecialNStart or ftCo_MS_CatchDash.
When item behavior depends on both a fighter owner and an item owner pointer, cleanup paths should explicitly clear the fighter's blaster reference and destroy associated effects.
Offset-named fields are acceptable as an intermediate step when semantics are not fully known, but grouping them into a named vars struct is still a major improvement over anonymous union access.
JObj transform tables are useful anchors for matching visual item effects; this PR tied float arrays to translate.z, scale.y/z, rotate.x, and translate.y calculations.
The PR body's "Not 100% diff yet" is a reminder to preserve uncertainty in downstream knowledge records and avoid treating all signatures or field meanings as final.
naming_conventions: Special-N enum name and values use ftFx_SpecialNIndex_* with action-like suffixes: Start, Loop, End, AirStart, AirLoop, AirEnd, ThrowB, ThrowHi, ThrowLw.
Unresolved struct fields retained offset-style names such as xDD4, xDD8, xE14, xE5C, xE74, xE78, and xE7C.
Unresolved functions retained address-style names such as it_802ADDD0, it_802AE8A8, it_802AEBCC, and it_802AF184.
Kirby copy helpers remained in the ftKb_SpecialNFx_800Fxxxx address-name convention, but their signatures were made more informative.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used address-named functions and data symbols to land a broad C translation before semantic renaming.
Preserved static/non-static uncertainty in comments such as /* static */ on forward declarations.
Used explicit float constants and arrays for transform data instead of recomputing or abstracting them away, supporting data-section matching.
Kept suspicious/generated-code comments and alternate code shapes, including M2C_ERROR notes and disabled expanded assignment blocks, which may help later matching iterations.
Used pointer-adjacent field copying in a loop for repeated blaster history fields, an apparent matching tactic compared with fully expanded assignments kept under #else.
Mapped item animation/state transitions through lookup tables it_803F6E3C and it_803F6E68 rather than hardcoding every branch.
Used Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and Item_802694CC to synchronize item animation with fighter/Kirby special-N action state.
Used direct HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ, HSD_JObjSetScaleY, HSD_JObjSetScaleZ, HSD_JObjSetRotationX, and HSD_JObjSetTranslateY calls to mirror observed JObj mutation patterns.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human PR body note was: "Not 100% diff yet", indicating the author knew the decomp was not fully matching/finished.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/it/items/itfoxblaster.c to see which functions became matching or were renamed.
Search for FoxBlasterVars field renames after PR 1427, especially xE74, xE78, and xE7C.
Check later changes to ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00, 800FDC70, 800FDD14, 800FDD4C, and 800FDDF4 for signature confirmation.
Look for PRs that remove M2C_ERROR comments or disabled #else assignment blocks in itfoxblaster.c.
Search whether .pre-commit-config.yaml remained in the repository or was adjusted after this PR.
